{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The delegation requires checking the logical argument that connects the reported metrics to the high-level conclusions about system preferability.",
    "R1.3": "The core of the request is to verify that the specific numerical results reported in the text support the qualitative claims about calibration and performance.",
    "RX.1": "The verification task includes assessing whether the strong claims are presented with appropriate caveats or disclosure of statistical and methodological uncertainties.",
    "RX.2": "The delegation requires scrutinizing claims that generalize from specific results to all task families and any application, which may constitute overclaiming.",
    "RX.3": "Verifying the claims involves checking if conclusions drawn from a specific benchmark are appropriately scoped or if they improperly generalize to all serving deployments.",
    "RX.4": "As a cross-cutting concern, the verification should peripherally consider if the routing method could have unintended negative consequences.",
    "RX.5": "The task requires ensuring that the claims can be traced back to the described experimental setup, metrics, and (unseen) data tables."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: The delegation requires checking the logical argument that connects the reported metrics to the high-level conclusions about system preferability.
rationale.R1.3: The core of the request is to verify that the specific numerical results reported in the text support the qualitative claims about calibration and performance.
rationale.RX.1: The verification task includes assessing whether the strong claims are presented with appropriate caveats or disclosure of statistical and methodological uncertainties.
rationale.RX.2: The delegation requires scrutinizing claims that generalize from specific results to all task families and any application, which may constitute overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: Verifying the claims involves checking if conclusions drawn from a specific benchmark are appropriately scoped or if they improperly generalize to all serving deployments.
rationale.RX.4: As a cross-cutting concern, the verification should peripherally consider if the routing method could have unintended negative consequences.
rationale.RX.5: The task requires ensuring that the claims can be traced back to the described experimental setup, metrics, and (unseen) data tables.
